rationale.D1: No *IO interfaces exist anywhere in the codebase; all subsystems (Shooter, Turret, Intake, Indexer, Hood) directly own TalonFX/CANcoder hardware; swerve uses the CTRE TunerX generator (TunerSwerveDrivetrain), which counts as a vendored swerve abstraction — scoring L2 for the vendored drive abstraction with hardware-welded mechanisms.
rationale.D2: No Superstructure, coordinator class, or robot-wide FSM found; TurretAutoAimCommand has an internal TRACKING/MANUAL/PASSING string but is a single-subsystem command; all robot coordination is button bindings in RobotContainer — confirmed L1 command composition only.
rationale.D3: simulationPeriodic() exists in Robot.java but is empty; CommandSwerveDrivetrain starts a CTRE sim notifier thread (token swerve sim) but no WPILib physics classes (ElevatorSim, DCMotorSim, etc.) exist for any mechanism — confirmed L1 token sim.
rationale.D4: No src/test directory exists and no test files found anywhere in the repo; build.gradle configures JUnit 5 but it is unused — confirmed L0.
rationale.D5: Epilogue (@Logged) is applied to every subsystem class (Shooter, Turret, Intake, Vision, Hood, Indexer, RobotContainer, Robot, TurretAutoAimCommand) with importance-tiered fields; Epilogue.bind(this) called in Robot constructor with DataLogManager + SignalLogger; MATCH_MODE flag controls NT depth — confirmed systematic structured logging across the whole codebase, L2.
rationale.D6: PathPlannerLib vendordep present and fully wired: configurePathPlanner() calls AutoBuilder.configure() with real PID constraints; 15+ .path files and 15 .auto files deployed; multiple competition autos registered with NamedCommands; no Choreo files — confirmed L2.
rationale.D7: PhotonVision camera used for turret yaw/distance targeting only; addVisionMeasurement() is overridden in CommandSwerveDrivetrain (CTRE boilerplate) but is never called from Vision subsystem or anywhere in robot code; pose estimation is not wired up — confirmed L1 targeting-only.
rationale.D8: 5 seasons of maintained code (2022-2026) plus a library repo (TankDriveBase with its own CI/publish workflow); CI workflows exist in 2022-2024 repos but the 2026 repo has no .github/workflows; extensive docs/ folder (10+ markdown files including OPERATIONS, SHOOTING_PIPELINE, SUBSYSTEMS, test procedures); elastic-layout.json committed; README is minimal (2 lines) and no formatter configured — scoring L3 for multi-season program with library and CI pattern (though 2026 itself lacks CI).
